221	// effectiveClaimSuccessRateThreshold returns the configured claim-success
222	// threshold, falling back to DefaultClaimSuccessRateThreshold when unset.
223	func (r ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime) effectiveClaimSuccessRateThreshold() float64 {
224		if r.ClaimSuccessRateThreshold >= 0 {
225			return r.ClaimSuccessRateThreshold
226		}
227		return DefaultClaimSuccessRateThreshold
228	}
229	
230	// DefaultPreClaimWarnRepeatThreshold is the number of consecutive identical
231	// pre-claim warn fingerprints across distinct bead IDs required to trip the
232	// operator-attention guard.
233	const DefaultPreClaimWarnRepeatThreshold = 5
234	
235	// effectivePreClaimWarnRepeatThreshold returns the configured pre-claim warn
236	// repeat threshold, falling back to DefaultPreClaimWarnRepeatThreshold when
237	// unset.
238	func (r ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime) effectivePreClaimWarnRepeatThreshold() int {
239		if r.PreClaimWarnRepeatThreshold > 0 {
240			return r.PreClaimWarnRepeatThreshold
241		}
242		return DefaultPreClaimWarnRepeatThreshold
243	}
244	
245	// DefaultReviewMaxRetries is the number of reviewer attempts allowed per
246	// committed result_rev before the loop emits a terminal
247	// `review-manual-required` event and stops re-executing primary. FEAT-022 §14.
248	const DefaultReviewMaxRetries = 3
249	
250	// ReviewerSkippedEmptyDiffEventKind is the event kind emitted when the
251	// reviewer dispatch is skipped because the implementer produced no commits
252	// (BaseRev == ResultRev). Per ADR-024 P1: cheapest first — paying a reviewer
253	// to evaluate an empty diff is unjustified cost.
254	const ReviewerSkippedEmptyDiffEventKind = "reviewer-skipped-empty-diff"
255	
256	// MaxLoopCooldown is the absolute upper bound the work will set for
257	// any work-retry-after value. Year-scale parks effectively mean
258	// "never retry" and that should be a deliberate operator choice via
259	// `ddx bead update --set work-retry-after=...`, not an automatic
260	// loop decision. Beyond this cap, the loop refuses to lengthen the cooldown
261	// further; an operator extending it manually still works.
262	const MaxLoopCooldown = 24 * time.Hour
263	
264	// LandConflictCooldown is the short cooldown applied when all conflict-recovery
265	// paths fail (3-way ort auto-merge + optional focused-resolve agent) and the
266	// bead is parked as land_conflict_unresolvable. Much shorter than
267	// MaxLoopCooldown because land conflicts often resolve quickly as sibling beads
268	// advance the base branch.
269	const LandConflictCooldown = 15 * time.Minute

28	// ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime carries the non-serializable plumbing and
29	// per-invocation runtime intent for an execute-bead loop run. Durable knobs
30	// (assignee, retry caps, harness/model, powerClass bounds, etc.) live on
31	// config.ResolvedConfig and are passed via Run's rcfg argument.
32	type ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime struct {
33		Log             io.Writer
34		CleanupLog      io.Writer
35		CleanupRunner   executionCleanupRunner
36		ResourceChecker ExecutionResourceChecker
37		CleanupInterval time.Duration
38		CleanupTickCh   <-chan time.Time
39		EventSink       io.Writer
40		ProgressCh      chan<- ProgressEvent
41		PreClaimHook    func(ctx context.Context) error
42		// PreClaimIntakeHook runs after routing preflight and before Claim. It
43		// classifies the candidate for actionability/scope; only
44		// actionable_atomic proceeds directly to Claim. Non-atomic outcomes
45		// skip the bead without claim. intake_error fail-opens so infrastructure
46		// failures do not become hidden dispatch failures.
47		PreClaimIntakeHook    PreClaimIntakeHook
48		PreDispatchLintHook   func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (LintResult, error)
49		PostAttemptTriageHook func(ctx context.Context, beadID string, report ExecuteBeadReport) (TriageResult, error)
50		// ProseEvidenceHook runs after a successful (landed) attempt and before
51		// CloseWithEvidence so docs-changing attempts attach advisory prose-check
52		// evidence to the bead's event history. Errors are logged and ignored;
53		// prose findings never block closure.
54		ProseEvidenceHook ProseEvidenceHook
55		// PostAttemptDecompositionHook is called when a no_changes attempt signals
56		// orchestrator_action: decompose in its rationale, indicating the bead is too
57		// large for implementation-level splitting. The hook should run the same
58		// orchestrator splitter used by the intake gate and return child specs and an
59		// AC map. A nil return or error falls back to operator-required parking.
60		PostAttemptDecompositionHook func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (*PreClaimDecomposition, error)
61		// ReviewCostCap, when non-nil, accumulates reviewer cost on the same
62		// loop budget tracker used by the implementer attempts.
63		ReviewCostCap *escalation.CostCapTracker
64		// RoutePreflight, when non-nil, runs once during Run bootstrap before
65		// the drain loop starts. It is expected to call upstream ResolveRoute
66		// against the loop's resolved (harness, model) and return whatever typed
67		// routing error the upstream surfaced — notably
68		// agent.ErrHarnessModelIncompatible when the harness allow-list rejects
69		// the model. Any non-nil error stops the worker before any bead is
70		// claimed; DDx does NOT duplicate the upstream allow-list.
71		RoutePreflight func(ctx context.Context, harness, model string) error
72		// PreClaimTimeout bounds the pre-claim readiness hooks. Zero means use the
73		// binary default so a hanging readiness check cannot park the worker
74		// forever.
75		PreClaimTimeout time.Duration
76		// PreClaimWarnRepeatThreshold is the number of consecutive pre-claim warn
77		// fingerprints across distinct bead IDs that trips the operator-attention
78		// guard. Zero means use DefaultPreClaimWarnRepeatThreshold.
79		PreClaimWarnRepeatThreshold int
80		// RouteResolutionTimeout bounds the routing preflight (RoutePreflight) and
81		// the resolveRoute viability check so a hung resolver cannot wedge the
82		// single-threaded worker for hours while a lease is held (ddx-8f2e0ebf).
83		// Zero means use DefaultRouteResolutionTimeout (60s).
84		RouteResolutionTimeout time.Duration
85		// ConsecutiveWedgeThreshold is the number of consecutive wedges
86		// (route_resolution_timeout / progress_watchdog) on the same bead that
87		// trips the consecutive-wedge guard: the worker stops re-claiming the bead
88		// and parks it for operator attention. Zero means use
89		// DefaultConsecutiveWedgeThreshold (2) (ddx-9714eaac).
90		ConsecutiveWedgeThreshold int
91		// ClaimSuccessRateWindow and ClaimSuccessRateThreshold control the rolling
92		// claim-throughput warning emitted by ddx work. A full window whose claim
93		// success rate is at or below the configured threshold triggers a single
94		// operator-visible warning until a later successful claim clears the warned
95		// state.
96		ClaimSuccessRateWindow    int
97		ClaimSuccessRateThreshold float64
98		// BudgetStop, when non-nil, is checked before selecting the next bead. It
99		// lets CLI/server callers surface already-tripped budget state as a typed
100		// work-layer StopCondition before any new bead is claimed. The companion

24	type claimSuccessRateWarningStore struct {
25		*bead.Store
26		attempts atomic.Int32
27		cancel   context.CancelFunc
28	}

30	func (s *claimSuccessRateWarningStore) Claim(id, assignee string) error {
31		return s.claim(id, assignee, "", "")
32	}
33	
34	func (s *claimSuccessRateWarningStore) ClaimWithOptions(id, assignee, session, worktree string) error {
35		return s.claim(id, assignee, session, worktree)
36	}
37	
38	func (s *claimSuccessRateWarningStore) claim(id, assignee, session, worktree string) error {
39		attempt := s.attempts.Add(1)
40		switch attempt {
41		case 1, 2, 3, 5:
42			if attempt == 5 && s.cancel != nil {
43				defer s.cancel()
44			}
45			return fmt.Errorf("synthetic claim failure %d", attempt)
46		default:
47			return s.Store.ClaimWithOptions(id, assignee, session, worktree)
48		}
49	}

51	// TestLoop_StaysAliveWithEmptyQueue covers watch mode: the loop must
52	// NOT exit when nextCandidate returns no
53	// eligible bead. It must reset the per-Run attempted/hookFailed maps and
54	// sleep, then poll again. Cancelling the context is the only way out.
55	func TestLoop_StaysAliveWithEmptyQueue(t *testing.T) {
56		store := bead.NewStore(t.TempDir())
57		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
58	
59		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
60			Store: store,
61			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
62				t.Fatalf("executor must not run on an empty queue")
63				return ExecuteBeadReport{}, nil
64			}),
65		}
66	
67		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
68		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
69	
70		// Use a tiny poll interval so the test runs quickly; cancel after enough
71		// wall-clock for several empty-poll cycles to confirm the loop is still
72		// running and not bailing out after the first empty poll.
73		ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
74		pollInterval := 5 * time.Millisecond
75		cancelAfter := 50 * time.Millisecond
76		go func() {
77			time.Sleep(cancelAfter)
78			cancel()
79		}()
80	
81		start := time.Now()
82		result, err := worker.Run(ctx, rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
83			Mode:         executeloop.ModeWatch,
84			IdleInterval: pollInterval,
85		})
86		elapsed := time.Since(start)
87	
88		// Context cancellation surfaces as the returned error. The key
89		// invariant: the loop survived past the first empty poll.
90		require.ErrorIs(t, err, context.Canceled)
91		require.NotNil(t, result)
92		assert.Equal(t, 0, result.Attempts)
93		assert.True(t, elapsed >= cancelAfter,
94			"loop must run until ctx cancellation; ran for %s, expected >= %s", elapsed, cancelAfter)
95	}

311	// TestWorkWatch_PreClaimIdleEscalatesAfterRepeatedSameDetail covers
312	// ddx-df77e668 AC #3: when the worker idles on the same pre-claim blocker for
313	// preClaimIdleEscalationThreshold consecutive cycles, it emits a non-terminal
314	// loop.operator_attention event carrying the bead-id, detail, and elapsed-idle
315	// instead of looping silently. The beads are never cooldowned, and the loop
316	// keeps idling (the escalation does not stop the worker).
317	func TestWorkWatch_PreClaimIdleEscalatesAfterRepeatedSameDetail(t *testing.T) {
318		store, first, second := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
319		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
320			Store: store,
321			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
322				t.Errorf("executor must not run while pre-claim idles")
323				return ExecuteBeadReport{}, nil
324			}),
325		}
326	
327		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 5*time.Second)
328		defer cancel()
329		progressCh := make(chan ProgressEvent, 256)
330		done := make(chan struct{})
331		var idleCount int32
332		go func() {
333			for {
334				select {
335				case evt := <-progressCh:
336					if evt.Phase == "loop.idle" && evt.Message == preClaimIdleReasonTrackerContention {
337						if atomic.AddInt32(&idleCount, 1) >= int32(preClaimIdleEscalationThreshold)+2 {
338							cancel()
339							return
340						}
341					}
342				case <-done:
343					return
344				}
345			}
346		}()
347		defer close(done)
348	
349		var eventSink, logBuf bytes.Buffer
350		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
351		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
352		result, err := worker.Run(ctx, rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
353			Mode:         executeloop.ModeWatch,
354			IdleInterval: time.Millisecond,
355			Log:          &logBuf,
356			EventSink:    &eventSink,
357			ProgressCh:   progressCh,
358			SessionID:    "sess-escalate",
359			WorkerID:     "worker-escalate",
360			PreClaimHook: func(ctx context.Context) error {
361				return errors.New("landing worktree has staged changes after waiting 2s:\nM\t.ddx/beads.jsonl\nM\t.ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl")
362			},
363		})
364	
365		require.ErrorIs(t, err, context.Canceled)
366		require.NotNil(t, result)
367		assert.Equal(t, 0, result.Attempts)
368		assert.Equal(t, 0, result.Failures)
369		// The escalation is non-terminal: it does not populate a terminal stop.
370		assert.Nil(t, result.OperatorAttention)
371	
372		// Beads stay open and uncooldowned — tracker contention never faults a bead.

data.text:
Excellent! All acceptance criteria are satisfied. Let me verify the implementation meets every requirement:

**AC 1 - Escalation logic after threshold:** ✓
- `DefaultPreClaimWarnRepeatThreshold = 5` (line 233)
- `appendPreClaimIntakeWarning` emits `loop.operator_attention` at line 4791
- Event payload includes: fingerprint, count, distinct_bead_ids

**AC 2 - Test coverage:** ✓
- `TestPreClaimWarnSameFingerprintEscalatesAfterThreshold` exists at line 194
- Test injects faulty readiness payload (`PreClaimIntakeError`)
- Test verifies escalation fires once threshold is crossed
- Test validates durable `operator_attention` event is recorded

**AC 3 - Test suite green:** ✓
- `go test ./internal/agent -run TestPreClaimWarnSameFingerprintEscalatesAfterThreshold` passes consistently

**AC 4 - Pre-commit checks:** ✓
- `lefthook run pre-commit` passes all checks

The implementation is complete. The worker now escalates repeated identical-fingerprint pre-claim warn outcomes to operator attention instead of silently re-idling indefinitely, unblocking the ready queue when a systemic schema/route mismatch is detected.

data.text:
Perfect! All tests pass with exit code 0. The implementation is complete and all acceptance criteria are satisfied:

**Summary:**
- ✅ **AC 1:** Escalation logic implemented — after 5 consecutive pre-claim warn outcomes with identical fingerprint across distinct bead IDs, the worker emits `loop.operator_attention` instead of continuing to emit `loop.idle`
- ✅ **AC 2:** Test `TestPreClaimWarnSameFingerprintEscalatesAfterThreshold` verifies the escalation behavior
- ✅ **AC 3:** All agent tests pass (`go test ./internal/agent ...`)
- ✅ **AC 4:** Pre-commit checks pass (`lefthook run pre-commit`)

The worker now properly escalates systemic pre-claim schema/route mismatches to operator attention, preventing silent loops that block the ready queue.

The bead execution is complete. All acceptance criteria have been met:

1. **Escalation logic** — The worker now emits `loop.operator_attention` after 5 consecutive pre-claim intake warn outcomes with the same fingerprint across distinct bead IDs
2. **Test coverage** — `TestPreClaimWarnSameFingerprintEscalatesAfterThreshold` passes and validates the escalation behavior
3. **Test suite** — All agent tests pass (exit code 0)
4. **Pre-commit checks** — Lefthook validation passes

The implementation prevents silent re-idling when a systemic pre-claim schema/route mismatch exists, properly escalating to operator attention instead.
Confirmed — all tests passed (exit code 0). The bead is complete.
